title: Epworth Literacy and Behaviour Intervention Programme
text:
Epworth Literacy and Behaviour Intervention Programme - SupportGoWhere
Epworth Literacy and Behaviour Intervention Programme
Share link
Offers literacy and behavioural intervention to support children aged 5 to 12 years old with learning difficulties.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Offers literacy and behavioural intervention to support children with literacy and behavioural challenges.

Services include:
* Literacy and/or language intervention - provides children between 5 to 12 years old with skills and strategies to overcome their learning challenges and develop literacy skills.
* Behavioural intervention - supports children between 5 to 12 years old in the aspects of essential executive functioning for learning, task readiness, on-seat behaviour, and basic social competency skills.
* Parenting workshops are also offered for parents to increase their knowledge in effective parenting and understanding the needs of their children.

Who is this for?

Children aged between 5 - 12 years old (in pre-schools and mainstream schools) with learning difficulties assessed to be behind their same-aged peers in their literacy and/or language skills.

What to expect?

Epworth will screen to assess if the child is eligible and suitable for the programme.

How to apply?

You may contact the service provider directly for enquiries and to sign up for the programme.

Information last updated on 29 Sep 2025
